abate.def: 減輕、減少、廢除、緩和、打折
abate.ex: The storm began to abate in the afternoon.

aberration.ex: The sudden drop in temperature was an aberration.

abhor.ex: Most people abhor violence against women.

abject.def: 極其卑賤的、卑躬屈膝的、令人絕望的、悲慘的
abject.ex: They were living in abject poverty.

abjure.def: 公開發誓放棄、棄絕、撤回
abjure.ex: He abjured his allegiance to the king.

ablution.ex: The priest performed his morning ablutions.

abnegation.ex: He is a man of remarkable self-abnegation.

abominate.ex: I abominate all forms of cruelty to animals.

aboriginal.def: 土著的、原始的、澳洲土著、土著者
aboriginal.ex: Aboriginal art is a significant part of Australian culture.

abortive.def: 失敗的、夭折的、發育不全的
abortive.ex: They made an abortive attempt to escape.

abrasive.def: 磨損的、有研磨作用的、傷人感情的、粗魯的、研磨劑
abrasive.ex: She has an abrasive personality that rubs people the wrong way.

abridge.ex: The book was abridged for younger readers.

abrogated.ex: The government has the power to abrogate the law.

abscission.def: 脫離、切斷、(植物)落葉時節
abscission.ex: Leaf abscission is a common occurrence in autumn.

abscond.ex: The cashier absconded with the company's funds.

absolve.def: 赦免、免除(罪、義務等)、宣佈無罪
absolve.ex: The court absolved him from any blame for the accident.

abstain.ex: He decided to abstain from alcohol for a month.

abstemious.def: 飲食有度的、節制的、適度的
abstemious.ex: She is known for her abstemious habits.

abstinence.ex: Total abstinence from tobacco is essential for health.

abstract.def: 抽象的、理論的、深奧的、摘要、抽象概念、提煉、總結、抽取地 (抽象)
abstract.col: abstract art, abstract concept, in the abstract
abstract.ex: Modern art is often very abstract.

abstruse.ex: The professor gave an abstruse explanation of the theory.

abysmal.def: 極壞的、糟糕透頂的、深不可測的

abyss.def: 深淵、地獄、極深的、深不可測的
abyss.ex: The climber looked down into the yawning abyss.

academic.def: 學術的、理論的、平庸的、大學教師、大學生、學究、學術地
academic.ex: The university is known for its high academic standards.

academy.ex: She is a student at the local film academy.

accede.ex: The government refused to accede to the demands of the strikers.

accelerate.ex: The car began to accelerate as it reached the highway.

accent.def: 語音、重音、特色、強調、著重說明、有重音的
accent.ex: He speaks with a strong German accent.

accept.ex: I'd be happy to accept your invitation.

acceptable.def: 可接受的、受歡迎的、滿意的
acceptable.ex: The noise level was barely acceptable.

acceptance.ex: The proposal met with widespread acceptance.

access.def: 進入、通道、接近、存取、使用、接近、進入、(電腦)存取、受保護的 (進入)
access.ex: The building has easy access for the disabled.

accessible.def: 可接近的、易受影響的、明白易懂的
accessible.ex: The information is readily accessible on the website.

accessory.def: 配件、從犯、附帶的、同謀的、副的、副本地
accessory.ex: She was wearing a lot of expensive fashion accessories.

accident.def: 意外、偶然、災害、事故、意外地
accident.col: traffic accident, fatal accident, by accident
accident.ex: He met her by accident in the street.

acclaim.def: 喝采、稱讚、宣佈、喝采、讚揚、喝采
acclaim.ex: The movie was released to great critical acclaim.

acclimatize.def: 適應新環境、使氣候適應
acclimatize.ex: It took them a week to acclimatize themselves to the high altitude.

acclivity.ex: The path followed a steep acclivity up the mountain.

accolade.def: 榮譽、讚揚、獲選、授予榮譽
accolade.ex: Winning the Nobel Prize is a high accolade for any scientist.

accommodate.def: 適應、調節、容納、提供住宿、調節、遷就
accommodate.ex: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.

accommodating.def: 樂於助人的、隨和的、適應性強的
accommodating.ex: The staff were very helpful and accommodating.

accommodation.ex: They are seeking temporary accommodation in the city.

accompany.ex: She accompanied him on the piano while he sang.

accomplice.ex: He was charged with being an unwitting accomplice to the crime.

accomplish.ex: They managed to accomplish the task in record time.

accomplished.def: 有才華的、熟練的、完成的、已實現的
accomplished.ex: She is an accomplished musician who plays several instruments.

accomplishment.ex: The publication of the dictionary was a great accomplishment.

accord.def: 一致、和諧、條約、給予、符合、給予、自願、一致地
accord.col: of one's own accord (自願), in accord with
accord.ex: The two countries reached a peace accord.

accordance.ex: The work was carried out in accordance with the instructions.

accordingly.def: 因此、於是、相應地、如實地
accordingly.ex: He was warned of the danger and acted accordingly.

accost.def: 搭訕、(不懷好意地)上前、招呼、攀談、勾引 (搭訕)
accost.ex: She was accosted by a stranger in the street.

account.def: 帳戶、說明、理由、計算、認為、把...視為、報帳、交代、有理由地 (帳戶)
account.col: bank account, take into account, on account of
account.ex: Please open a bank account for me.

accountability.ex: There is a need for greater public accountability in the government.

accountable.def: 負有責任的、應作說明的、可說明的
accountable.ex: Parents should be held accountable for their children's behavior.

accountant.ex: He works as a chartered accountant for a big firm.

accounting.ex: She is studying accounting at the university.

accredit.def: 授權、信任、鑑定...為合格、歸因於
accredit.ex: The hospital was accredited by the international commission.

accretion.ex: The land was formed by the gradual accretion of silt.

accrue.ex: Interest will accrue on the savings account.

accumulate.ex: He managed to accumulate a lot of wealth over the years.

accumulation.ex: There was a vast accumulation of dust in the attic.

accuracy.ex: The report was checked for technical accuracy.

accurate.ex: The map is not very accurate.

accurately.ex: It's difficult to predict the future accurately.

accursed.def: 被詛咒的、不幸的、令人討厭的
accursed.ex: That accursed car has broken down again.

accusation.ex: He denied the accusation of theft.

accuser.ex: He faced his accuser in the court.

accustom.ex: It took some time to accustom himself to the new job.

accustomed.ex: I am not accustomed to such hot weather.

ace.def: 么點牌、好手、網球發球得分、著名的、王牌的、一擊得分、贏過、在網球中發球得分
ace.col: ace in the hole (壓箱寶), within an ace of

acerbic.ex: She is known for her acerbic wit.

acerbity.ex: There was a lot of acerbity in his voice.

ache.def: 疼痛、渴望、渴望的人 (或事)、痛、受苦、渴望、痛地 (痛)
ache.ex: My head aches from all the noise.

achievable.ex: Setting achievable goals is the key to success.

achieve.ex: They managed to achieve success in the end.

achievement.ex: The publication of the dictionary was an outstanding achievement.

acid.def: 酸性的、尖刻的、苦澀的、酸、迷幻藥、(化學)酸

acidify.ex: Pollution can acidify the rain water.

acidity.ex: Antacids can help to reduce stomach acidity.

acknowledge.ex: He refused to acknowledge his mistake.

acknowledgment.ex: She received public acknowledgment for her contributions.

acme.ex: The performance was the acme of perfection.

acoustic.def: 聽覺的、原聲的、聲學的、原聲吉他、助聽器、聲學地
acoustic.ex: He is playing an acoustic guitar.

acoustics.ex: The concert hall has excellent acoustics.

acquaint.ex: It took some time to acquaint himself with the new system.

acquaintance.ex: He is a casual acquaintance of mine.

acquainted.ex: Are you acquainted with his work?

acquiesce.ex: He reluctantly acquiesced in the decision.

acquiescence.ex: The plan was carried out with the silent acquiescence of the staff.

acquire.def: 獲得、取得、學到、捕獲 (獲得)
acquire.ex: He managed to acquire a lot of knowledge over the years.

acquired.def: 後天的、習得的、已獲得的
acquired.ex: Blue cheese is often an acquired taste.

acquisition.def: 獲得、收購、收購之物、才幹
acquisition.ex: The museum's most recent acquisition is a famous painting.

acquisitive.def: 好學的、貪得無厭的、貪得的
acquisitive.ex: We live in a very acquisitive society.
